playerClass.name: Bard
playerClass.description: Your skill with spreading a good tale allows you to spread story outcomes to all players, including yourself

playerClass.name: Herald
playerClass.description: Your rank allows you to give new titles to other players

playerClass.name: Scribe
playerClass.description: Your fondness for recording the histories of other adventurers allows you to add Traits to other players

playerClass.name: Fabulist
playerClass.description: Your penchant for writing fables allows you to convert Encounters players may have into Traits that stay with them

options.attemptText: You spend the week planting and caring for the plant life. Your standing with nature improves.
You spend the tending to the beasts and retrieving their pelts and milks. Your body hardens and you get to pet lots of cute critters.

gameSessionDisplay.mapDescription: The season of harvest has arrived and we all must spend the coming weeks preparing for the ritual to our patron God of Harvest, Erysus. Our very survival is dependent on her whims.
gameSessionDisplay.goalDescription: The ritual at the end of the season will determine whether we will live or die this year. Those who are invested in the well-being of our little hamlet will make choices that please Erysus, but there will still be those among us who choose frivolity.
gameSessionDisplay.endingDescription: Erysus arrives. Her great blue eye drifts behind the clouds that make up the harvest sky, expectantly. We must all do our part to make sure our ritual to her pleases her. How will we each proceed?
gameSessionDisplay.successText: And so the ritual lasted long into the night just as it always does. All of the citizens of our little hamlet in the middle of Nowhere did their best to impress Erysus lest she deliver a season where they could provide for their families. After a night of colors and feasting and prayer, we awaken to fields abundant with new sprouts. The winter has ended and Erysus has looked kindly upon us. We hug and we cry and we begin to work again once more in the fields and those among us who believe set upon their task of preparing to pray again next year before the god who sees our faith and shows us mercy. The almighty Erysus!
gameSessionDisplay.neutralText: And so the ritual lasted long into the night just as it always does. All of the citizens of our little hamlet in the middle of Nowhere did their best to impress Erysus lest she deliver a season where they could provide for their families. It was a long night and some things went better than others and as the sun rises it is unclear how we did. The wind is hollow and the dirt does not seem to bare fruit. Just as the townspeople are preparing to pack up their things and scramble to the local trading post in anticipation of a bare season, someone notices a small patch of crop peaking up from behind the pyre. We may not have excelled, but the glimmer of Erysus' eye shines down upon us. Perhaps we will not starve after all.
gameSessionDisplay.failureText: And so the ritual lasted long into the night just as it always does. All of the citizens of our little hamlet in the middle of Nowhere did their best to impress Erysus lest she deliver a season where they could provide for their families. However. The night is filled with folly and, despite the best efforts of the faithful, Erysus seems to have abandoned us come morning. The fields do not sprout, the air remains cold, and children go hungry. The season of winter has ended, but we do not see any change. Those who are faithful begin their task of convincing the community that our ritual next year should be grander, but many in the community lose faith altogether. It will be a year of despair.

options.attemptText: You spend the week drinking. You gain some friends and build a tolerance.
You spend the week serving mead. You are charming and are tipped well!
You spend the week planting and caring for the plant life. Your standing with nature improves.
You spend the tending to the beasts and retrieving their pelts and milks. Your body hardens and you get to pet lots of cute critters.
You spend the week contributing to the town's systems of governance. The rigorous discussion improves your ability to make your voice heard.
You spend the week learning about the town's history. You feel better prepared for the road ahead.
You spend the week wandering in the wilds and avoiding danger. Your footing improves and you even find some treasure.
You spend the week looking for resources for the town. You learn a lot about the forest surrounding your town!
You spend the week training under the masters of the old magics. You learn a lot and feel your connection to the world increase.
You spend the week trying out spells you haven't tried before. You grow your familiarity with magic and see some unexpected results.
You dance in the center of the town square for coin. People enjoy your energy and some even drop some coin!
You spend the week serving mead to the locals. You make some coin and put in some hard work.
You spend the week in pious reverie. You feel the glow and self-satisfaction of knowing you are in good standing with your god.
You spend the week helping to erect the display for the harvest ritual. Your body is hardened by the good work and your soul is enriched.

options.successText: Your sense of flow and natural athletic artistry spurs the town into a magnificent choreographed display!
options.failureText: The other dancers appreciate your efforts, but there is only so many times you can step on other people's toes before they move on to other tasks.

options.successText: The feast is perfectly balanced, nourishing both body and spirit.
options.failureText: The offerings feel lacking, diminishing the ritual’s blessing.

options.successText: Your antics bring unexpected wisdom through humor!
options.failureText: Your jokes fall flat, and confusion ensues.

options.successText: The runes glow with power, enhancing the ritual’s intent.
options.failureText: The symbols lack clarity, and their power falters.

options.successText: The god's presence fills the space, empowering all present.
options.failureText: Your voice falters, and the connection feels weak.

options.successText: Your cunning and agility keep the ceremony lively!
options.failureText: Your antics cause more chaos than intended.

options.successText: Your eloquence ensures the ritual runs smoothly and inspires awe!
options.failureText: Your words falter, and the crowd grows restless.

options.successText: The stones resonate with energy, forming a potent circle.
options.failureText: The placements feel unbalanced, weakening the ritual.

options.attemptText: You inscribe sacred runes to channel energy.
options.successText: The runes glow with power, enhancing the ritual’s intent.
options.failureText: The symbols lack clarity, and their power falters.

options.attemptText: You oversee the sacred meal, ensuring offerings are honored.
options.successText: The feast is perfectly balanced, nourishing both body and spirit.
options.failureText: The offerings feel lacking, diminishing the ritual’s blessing.

options.attemptText: You call upon the presence of the god Erysus.
options.successText: The god's presence fills the space, empowering all present.
options.failureText: Your voice falters, and the connection feels weak.

options.successText: You dance the border of worlds, bridging the realms.
options.failureText: The spirits remain distant, their whispers unheard.

options.successText: A clear vision emerges, guiding the town's future.
options.failureText: Your vision is clouded, leaving uncertainty.

options.successText: The flames blaze high, illuminating the ritual with power.
options.failureText: The fire sputters, casting uncertain shadows.

options.attemptText: You lead the rhythmic chants, setting the ritual's tone.
options.successText: Your powerful voice resonates, guiding the ritual to new heights!
options.failureText: Your voice wavers, and the chant loses its rhythm.

options.attemptText: You work to gather the other dancers around the pyre.
